item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2011 called, and they said "No, you're not cool".

Seriously, I thought this trend died with the Obama administration. I was ready to upvote until I read that stupid, tacked-on line. And like others have said, this doesn't fit AWCY?'s Bullshit High-Art-Is-Incomprehensible Modus Operandi. They worship Andy Warhol, they'd be making art mocking this kind of cartoon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
PoI-5019 submitted the clip to her boss, and SCP-5019 suddenly manifested. PoI-6786 is unaware as to how her messing with the episode spread such an effect, but holds clear guilt as she explained the situation to fellow members of her artists' cabal.

This, among other bits, strikes me as egregiously nonclinical in tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed, thanks for pointing that out! And I'm glad!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
PoI-6786 admitted creating a finished clip for the episode, and to implementing it as a part of PoI-6786's work.

Shouldn't it be "as a part of PoI-5019's work."

Other than that, I liked it, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much! I’m glad you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good article, good story, really enjoyable, +1 with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I wasn't aware of that. I just used it cause I liked it, I can change it. Thanks for letting me know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get why this has the special The Foundation Theme or what narrative purpose it serves. It's not like this is a simple shift like the regular Black Highlighter is, and actively breaks multiple elements of this page (the link hovers put two weird little bars next to every image and mess with the LostMedia wiki section).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this article enough to upvote it, but it's far from perfect. The AWCY reference didn't do that much for me, but it didn't ruin the article in any regard, and for such a hard GOI to reference well I think you pulled it off pretty decently. Overall I had already grasped the theme from what you had written and the explanation at the end wasn't really necessary, but it was well written enough that I guess I didn't mind it.

Anyways, one thing you might wanna touch up: "PoI-6786 is unaware as to how her messing with the episode spread such an effect, but holds clear guilt as she explained the situation to fellow members of her artists' cabal." - I don't like how "messing with" sounds here, maybe change it to "tampering with"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
but what I want to know is, where's the caveman?

first actual interesting "straight" AWCY article in years +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was genuinely interesting. The fact that you drew/photoshopped a bunch of pictures and art for this SCP hooked me in instantly to read the rest of the article. The amount of care that you put into this article, especially manipulating css (which most people don’t do nowadays,) shows that I can expect more good articles from you in the future.

tl;dr: Good article. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still thought it was cool. It didn't really fit the AWCY but the effort was put into this SCP and overall it was okay. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know I'm all about creepy cartoons as a concept, but I feel like this one doesn't hit the mark. Formatting without a strong, narrative-driven reason for it always irks me, and a lot of the segments here like they'd be just as effective written out plainly. And, thought everyone else has said it, this doesn't feel like AWCY - it's just not their M.O.

That said, I see the amount of work put in here. I loved all the artistic elements, but I would have liked them better if they were part of a more cohesive article. Novote, for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmmm…. I find the central idea of a SCP that symbolizes being unable to get one's original vision across to others because they just see it their own way while ignoring one's original goal interesting.

Nonetheless, to echo others, the AWCY? reference at the end is rather gregarious.

And I also wonder if some of the examples could be trimmed out.

It's a good SCP, though, just need some more work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: gonna have to agree, yeah

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd go further than tacked on and say that the AWCY connection felt out of character for the group. To my mind, AWCY are mainly defined by a culture of 'high art' - appreciation for classical styles and postmodern innovation, installations displayed in exclusive galleries, making a statement with their work. Working for an animated television series aimed at a youth audience feels very far from that scene. I think distilling things down to "drawings = artwork = AWCY" is an oversimplification, without consideration for the mediums or culture.

That it comes out of nowhere right at the end kind of compounds the feeling that the mention lacks much justification for being there at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for your feedback! Yeah I was wondering about the risk class and stuff. But it's Keter because it can't be contained. Part of the reason they have a hard time containing it is to fit the the theme of entitlement and censorship.

I could think over a bit different ending, but I'm pretty contempt with what it is right now. Maybe in the future I'll find ways to edit it a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The AWCY bit was actually a bit tacked on, I was given the idea for it, it wasn't part of the original draft. The media bits were to try to build up the world a bit more, but I guess I see how it came off that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, I found this article to be enjoyable, and it has quite the potential indeed. I don’t think this is a bad piece, but I feel it still has some stuff that needs addressing.
Firstly, I don’t really get the object classification. I don’t see a reason for this SCP to be a Keter, and for the Foundation to have such a hard time redacting and censoring it. The risk class and notice class also seem a bit off; In my humble reader opinion, I think you should either make the object more containable or make the relation between the whole “entitlement and censorship” theme and the anomalous properties more obvious and logical to us readers.
Also, just like gee0765 pointed out earlier, the ending is quite underwhelming. The AWCY? bit seems really artificial and lame. Maybe you could change the ending to focus more on the relationship between the two PoI, and an eventual fallout and maybe suicide as cause of the anomalous behavior. ( Note: this is just something I came up with on a whim. I’m certain you can probably figure something that better fits your style and objectives)
In conclusion, I must say that while this article is still not SCP Foundation quality, its not THAT far from it. Anyway, this is all just this newbie’s opinion. I really hope your SCP grows and develops exponentially, DianaBerry!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, all the media bits didn't really do that much for me. They only really existed to reiterate what had been set out in the paragraph before, and as an excuse to put some art in. Since the characters set out in them didn't really show up again, they went on far longer than they deserved. There's also a fair few grammatical/spelling/tonal errors throughout the piece.

The final messages were entirely lacking in any form of subtlety. The Twitter thread only served to tell the reader about this character and why she made the episode, without leaving anything up to the reader to work it out. Being practically hit on the head with the intentions without any room for interpretations made the entire message feel extremely forced.

Finally, as is extremely common for this group of interest, the AWCY? link at the end felt extremely tacked on. It attempts to introduce a new character with new motives which aren't developed on much at all in the time, for what felt like just an excuse to jam in the final image and the connection to the group. I understand that it serves as an explanation for the anomalous effects; however, it's both unclear how or why these specific effects have manifested, something which is only explained with a 'they don't know'.

item.title: Author's Post
item.content:encoded:
So it's been awhile since I posted a scip, eh? I guess it's just hard to find what works. This idea originally was something I had in the format of a parawatch tale, but Calibri Bold confirmed that it didn't work in that format. So I changed up some things, and now here it is as an SCP!

This scip has my feelings in it in some places, so I guess it means a good bit to me

All art is done by me. All profile pictures used are either art pieces I did, or pictures I took. One is of my cat, one is of ice-cream I had one time. If you want to see the full images I'll show you, but they're mine lol.

Special thanks to my reviwers/betareaders:

  • MalyceGraves-Gave me the idea for the AWCY bit and final addendum, and with wording and flow in general.
  • Mew-ltiverse-Helped me find the AWCY direction once the idea was pitched.
  • BlueJones
  • Calibri Bold
  • DrMoned
  • drferrum
  • Doctor Fullham

Special thanks to Woedenaz who made me the lost media wiki comment code, I owe you my life dude, and djkaktus who helped my dumb stubborn ass with formatting the last two images.

Hope y'all enjoyed! It only took like a week to whip up, but it also took some hard work and dedication. Until next time!~

Edit: If you’re on mobile, the images are gonna look weird. I’ll try to fix that tomorrow but I’m tired for tonight.
